text:
Once upon a time on an island secluded by moss-covered rocks, there was a gentle breeze whispering tales of adventure. The island's entrance was guarded by a giant oak tree, its branches rustling with secrets. Inside that tree lived an old owl named Olga. Olga hadn't hooted for weeks.

One sunny morning, curious about the world beyond her silent vigil, Olga decided to take flight. The island breeze cheered her on as she fluttered across waves. After many days of journeying, she paused near some quiet cliffs that appeared untouched by time.

There stood an ancient stone circle, its stones whispering stories of stars long departed. Olga perched atop one stone—a circle stone herself—and listened to their nostalgic chorus.

In the circle, five stones gleamed with hidden runes — symbols of magic. Olga fluttered closer, sensing their power. She hooted softly as she approached.

Suddenly, shadows shifted. From the mist arose a curious beast—a fox
